title: Beating The Market With Just 5 REITs
text: We discuss a diversified basket of five REITs that have historically outperformed the market and exhibited subdued volatility. These five REITs include cell tower, data center, retail, apartment, and industrial assets. The basket has delivered a higher annual return (13.9%) compared to the S&P 500 (9.5%) and the Vanguard Real Estate (6.7%), while also having lower volatility.

title: 5 REITs: Mixing Good And Bad
text: Some REITs are good, some are bad, and most face challenges from rising interest rates. Good REITs have traits that lead to solid long-term returns, such as consistent revenue growth and the ability to funnel that growth into AFFO per share. Specific REITs mentioned include Hudson Pacific Properties, Terreno Realty, American Homes 4 Rent, Mid-America Apartment Communities, and Industrial Logistics Properties Trust.

title: The Answer Is 7
text: Cap rates are expected to move back to higher historical levels, potentially higher than the current 6% or 7%. The risk-free interest rate, the 10-year U.S. Treasury Bond yield, is a key reference point for evaluating real estate investments. Realty Income and Mid-America Apartment Communities are two REITs that are expected to perform well.

title: Never Forget: REITs Own Property Run By People
text: REIT stocks have not performed well this year, but the quality of the management team is an important factor in determining the success of a REIT. Examples of REITs with strong management teams include Realty Income, Prologis, Alexandria Real Estate, Rexford Industrial, Mid-America Apartment, and Camden Property Trust. These REITs have experienced positive AFFO growth and have strong balance sheets, making them attractive investment opportunities.

title: Want $1 Million in Retirement? 3 Stocks to Buy Now and Hold for Decades
text: Brookfield Renewable should continue growing briskly as it expands its clean energy portfolio. Blackstone is tapping into another massive market opportunity.

title: Lessons Learned: Adversity Is Bitter But Its Uses May Be Sweet
text: I reflect on my past experiences as a real estate developer and the financial collapse I faced. Investing in quality properties that provide long-term value is of utmost importance. Emphasis must be placed on the significance of quality balance sheets and management teams in successful investing.

title: Mid-America Apartment And Camden Property: Undervalued, Good Time To Buy
text: Both Mid-America Apartment and Camden Property Trust are trading below their 5-year P/AFFO average, making both attractive right now. MAA has a slightly better balance sheet, superior total returns vs the S&P, and a longer dividend growth streak. Both have similar occupancy ratings but could see an uptick in vacancies if the country does enter into a recession.

title: Is Mid-America Apartment (MAA) an Apt Portfolio Pick Right Now?
text: Mid-America Apartment (MAA) to gain from a well-diversified Sun Belt-focused portfolio, a strong development pipeline, and redevelopment and technology initiatives despite high interest rates.

title: Bloodshed On REIT Street: 5 High-Quality Landlords I'm Buying For The Long Haul
text: Larger REITs have outperformed small- and mid-sized REITs in the last five years, thanks partially to their concentration in high-growth sectors. Modestly leveraged REITs can still grow their bottom-line profits even with rapidly rising interest expenses because revenue is growing as well. I highlight five high-quality, larger market cap REITs that are currently trading at reasonable (or better-than-reasonable) valuations. I'm buying for the long haul.

title: REIT Income For Life
text: We emphasize the value of reading and considering different perspectives, even if they don't align with one's own beliefs. This article highlights comments from readers expressing concerns about the state of the US economy and consumer debt. We recommend considering real estate investment trusts such as Realty Income, Mid-America Apartment Communities, Agree Realty, and VICI Properties for consistent dividend payments and potential long-term returns.

title: Mid-America Apartment Communities: Sunbelt Multifamily Properties May Not Remain So Resilient
text: The REIT sector has suffered the worst performance in the stock market, with a decline of ~23% in the past year due to the sharp rise in mortgage rates. Multifamily REITs are performing better than office REITs due to rent growth outpacing inflation over the past decade, driven by low home affordability and family formation rates. Mid-America Apartment Communities is a potential discount investment opportunity, with a decline of over 30% since early 2022.

title: Got $1,000? Generate Real Estate Income the Easy Way With These Investments
text: Invitation Homes offers an easier way to invest in single-family rental properties. MAA enables investors to earn dividend income backed by rents from a large portfolio of apartment communities.

title: Mid-America Apartment Communities: Attractively Priced With A Fortress Balance Sheet
text: Mid-America Apartment Communities is a Sunbelt multifamily pure-play. The company has one of the best balance sheets of all REITs and has seen a tremendous quarter with double-digit YoY growth. I present my buy thesis and suggest an alternative Sunbelt REIT.

title: Government Finances Are A Mess: Focus On Quality
text: Fitch Ratings downgraded the US Federal Government from AAA to AA+, causing the stock market to roil. Real estate investment trusts (REITs) have fared better than tech stocks in the day after the downgrade announcement, as REITs were already relatively cheap. While the downgrade itself is virtually meaningless, it does spur us to look at the US Government's finances.
